structural_claim: Duality principles reveal that seemingly different structural perspectives are equivalent, with geometric and analytic viewpoints being two sides of the same mathematical reality

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Geometric structure (inner products) and analytic structure (continuous linear functionals) are equivalent through canonical duality
Algebraic structure (field extensions) and arithmetic structure (ideal class groups) are equivalent through canonical correspondence
Galois representations and automorphic representations are proposed to be equivalent, unifying algebraic and analytic perspectives
